explanations.summary: Project X moves from day 5 at 16:00 to day 3 at 14:00 because its tasks are scheduled ahead of lower-priority portfolio work while preserving explicit dependencies and task eligibility.

Jill is explicitly eligible for Project X assembly work, but her approved PTO on days 1 and 2 removes the earliest execution window. Alex is selected because Alex is explicitly eligible and can complete the task sooner without violating Jill's protected availability.
Project Y completes 2820 minutes later because shared staff capacity is reassigned to Project X.
Project Z completes 2760 minutes later because shared staff capacity is reassigned to Project X.
The scenario uses bounded overtime (Alex: 60 minutes, Sam: 60 minutes). The synthetic overtime compensation is $127.50, including an incremental premium of $42.50.
